title: baphia multiflora subsp. multiflora
intro: Baphia multiflora subsp. multiflora, commonly known as the "Tree Marigold," is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Baphia multiflora subsp. multiflora has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, treating skin conditions, and preventing infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as decoctions and infusions, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
